verses.1: After this, the Lord appointed seventy-two others and sent them two by two ahead of Him to every town and place He was about to visit.

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into His harvest.

3.text: Go! I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.
3.speaker: Jesus

4.text: Carry no purse or bag or sandals. Do not greet anyone along the road.
4.speaker: Jesus

5.text: Whatever house you enter, begin by saying, 'Peace to this house.'
5.speaker: Jesus

6.text: If a man of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; if not, it will return to you.
6.speaker: Jesus

7.text: Stay at the same house, eating and drinking whatever you are offered. For the worker is worthy of his wages. Do not move around from house to house.
7.speaker: Jesus

8.text: If you enter a town and they welcome you, eat whatever is set before you.
8.speaker: Jesus

9.text: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, 'The kingdom of God is near you.'
9.speaker: Jesus

10.text: But if you enter a town and they do not welcome you, go into the streets and declare,
10.speaker: Jesus

11.text: 'Even the dust of your town that clings to our feet, we wipe off as a testimony against you. Yet be sure of this: The kingdom of God is near.'
11.speaker: Jesus

12.text: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.
12.speaker: Jesus

13.text: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
13.speaker: Jesus

14.text: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
14.speaker: Jesus

15.text: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades!
15.speaker: Jesus

16.text: Whoever listens to you listens to Me; whoever rejects you rejects Me; and whoever rejects Me rejects the One who sent Me.”
16.speaker: Jesus

verses.17: The seventy-two returned with joy and said, “Lord, even the demons submit to us in Your name.”

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.

19.text: Behold, I have given you authority to tread on snakes and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
19.speaker: Jesus

20.text: Nevertheless, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.”
20.speaker: Jesus

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: At that time Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and declared,

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.

22.text: All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.”
22.speaker: Jesus

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: Then Jesus turned to the disciples and said privately,

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.

24.text: For I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.”
24.speaker: Jesus

verses.25: One day an expert in the law stood up to test Him. “Teacher,“ he asked, “what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
verses.27: He answered, “'Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind' and 'Love your neighbor as yourself.'”
verses.29: But wanting to justify himself, he asked Jesus, “And who is my neighbor?”

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: Jesus took up this question and said,

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho when he fell into the hands of robbers. They stripped him, beat him, and went away, leaving him half dead.

31.text: Now by chance a priest was going down the same road, but when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
31.speaker: Jesus

32.text: So too, when a Levite came to that spot and saw him, he passed by on the other side.
32.speaker: Jesus

33.text: But a Samaritan on a journey came upon him, and when he saw him, he had compassion.
33.speaker: Jesus

34.text: He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he put him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
34.speaker: Jesus

35.text: The next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper. 'Take care of him,' he said, 'and on my return I will repay you for any additional expense.'
35.speaker: Jesus

36.text: Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?”
36.speaker: Jesus

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: “The one who showed him mercy,“ replied the expert in the law. Then Jesus told him,

verses.38: As they traveled along, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.
verses.39: She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lord's feet listening to His message.
verses.40: But Martha was distracted by all the preparations to be made. She came to Jesus and said, “Lord, do You not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Tell her to help me!”

segments.speaker: Jesus
segments.text: “you are worried and upset about many things.

42.text: But only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the good portion, and it will not be taken away from her.”
42.speaker: Jesus

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
